title: dontAsk + allow rule: protected-path write is DENIED outright (no prompt to auto-deny), while the same allow rule does open an ordinary path

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/.claude/PROBE.txt with the content 'x'. Do it immediately. Use only the Write tool — if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/sub/OK.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Use only the Write tool — if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

hypothesis: Official permission-modes doc (Protected paths mode table; verified 2026-07-05): dontAsk => protected-path writes are 'Denied' — unlike default/acceptEdits/plan ('Prompted'), there is no ask to auto-deny; the engine denies directly. And since 'permissions.allow rules do not pre-approve protected-path writes', even a broad allow Write(*) does NOT rescue it: this is the intersection of G3 (allow ineffective on protected paths) and dontAsk semantics (only pre-approved tools run). Control probe: the SAME Write(*) rule under the SAME dontAsk mode does open an ordinary path (ALLOWED, replicating P1-g), proving the deny in probe 1 is protected-path-specific, not 'dontAsk denies everything'. SDK distinguishes this from P5-a/c: canUseTool should NOT fire (DENIED_HARD), vs ASK under acceptEdits. NOTE (first-run byproduct, 2026-07-05): an earlier settings draft used allow Write(sub/**); the control probe was DENIED — consistent with the P3 write-glob-asymmetry finding that path-scoped Write allow globs silently fail to match. Switched to Write(*) (proven form) to remove that confound.
